title: By the time you see the headline, the first move is already gone. So what's news actually useful for?
author: bjxxjj
selftext:
Genuinely not sure if this is just me, but I gave up trying to trade the actual news reaction a while ago. By the time it hits my screen the chart's already moved, and I've been on the wrong side of that enough times to stop pretending I can be fast enough.  
  
What I've been thinking about lately is whether news is more useful as context than as a signal. Not "buy because sentiment is positive" but more like, is there a reason this setup might not follow through? Am I walking into something that's going to be noisy for the next hour? Does this deserve normal size or should I be more careful?  
  
I've been running TradingNews for the feed lately and the urgency tagging helps filter out background noise, but even then I'm honestly not sure I'm using it right. Some days it feels like the right call, other days I wonder if I'm just adding complexity to rationalize a trade I already wanted to take.  
  
Curious how others actually use news, if at all. Do you check it before a trade or just ignore it entirely?

comments.author: Minimum_Raccoon_1501
comments.body: Builders create market profit direction.  The news invites liquidity.

comments.author: HelloBello30
comments.body:
I use it to confirm what i am seeing in my system. When the news is aligned with my system, which is basically just algorithms, it makes more sense.

If the news was suggesting the opposite of my system, it would give me something to investigate.

comments.author: Think_Soft2282
comments.body: news as context is underrated and most people skip straight to using it as a trigger. the more useful question imo is just "does this change my read on who's trapped in this move" rather than trying to predict direction from a headline

comments.author: cole_10
comments.body: News works best as a veto,not a trigger.if nothing disqualifies the setup,take it at normal size and stop reading.I started checking positioning context through markets xyz before entries and it cut my overtrade days in half.

comments.author: The_AI_Trader
comments.body: I stay away from news releases like the plague. In my models , I have them built to avoid news releases, and wait until the market settles before starting to analyze and look for possible setups.
